2604.11835.title: Schema-Adaptive Tabular Representation Learning with LLMs for Generalizable Multimodal Clinical Reasoning
2604.11835.paper_summary: Machine learning for tabular data remains constrained by poor schema generalization, a challenge rooted in the lack of semantic understanding of structured variables. This challenge is particularly acute in domains like clinical medicine, where electronic health record (EHR) schemas vary significantly. To solve this problem, we propose Schema-Adaptive Tabular Representation Learning, a novel method that leverages large language models (LLMs) to create transferable tabular embeddings. By transforming structured variables into semantic natural language statements and encoding them with a pretrained LLM, our approach enables zero-shot alignment across unseen schemas without manual feature engineering or retraining. We integrate our encoder into a multimodal framework for dementia diagnosis, combining tabular and MRI data. Experiments on NACC and ADNI datasets demonstrate state-of-the-art performance and successful zero-shot transfer to unseen schemas, significantly outperforming clinical baselines, including board-certified neurologists, in retrospective diagnostic tasks. These results validate our LLM-driven approach as a scalable, robust solution for heterogeneous real-world data, offering a pathway to extend LLM-based reasoning to structured domains.
2604.11835.paper_summary_zh: 機器學習在表格數據上的應用仍受限於糟糕的模式泛化，這一挑戰根源於對結構變量缺乏語義理解。這一挑戰在臨床醫學等領域尤為明顯，因為電子健康記錄（EHR）模式差異顯著。為了解決這一問題，我們提出了模式自適應表格表示學習，這是一種新穎的方法，利用大型語言模型（LLMs）來創建可轉移的表格嵌入。通過將結構變量轉換為語義自然語言陳述並使用預訓練的LLM進行編碼，我們的方法實現了在未見模式之間的零樣本對齊，而無需手動特徵工程或重新訓練。我們將編碼器整合到一個多模態框架中，用於癡呆症診斷，結合了表格數據和MRI數據。在NACC和ADNI數據集上的實驗顯示出最先進的性能，並成功實現了對未見模式的零樣本轉移，在回顧性診斷任務中顯著超越了臨床基準，包括經認證的神經科醫生。這些結果驗證了我們基於LLM的方法作為一種可擴展、穩健的解決方案，適用於異質的現實世界數據，為將基於LLM的推理擴展到結構化領域提供了途徑。
2604.11835.author: Hongxi Mao et.al.
2604.11835.authors: Hongxi Mao, Wei Zhou, Mengting Jia, Tao Fang, Huan Gao, Bin Zhang, Shangyang Li
